title: Leionema beckleri
taxonomy: Leionema beckleri is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to Australia and is also known as a part of the citrus family. The classification of Leionema beckleri is further divided into its genus and species levels within the Rutaceae family. Leionema beckleri is a part of the tribe Boronieae and is closely related to other species within this tribe. The exact classification of Leionema beckleri is further detailed within the APG III plant classification system.

intro: Leionema beckleri, also known as Beckler's leionema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to eastern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine and has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Leionema beckleri include flavonoids, coumarins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Leionema beckleri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits in traditional medicine.
